H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) achieves 66 % positive outcomes, the highest‑n HEALING protocol. Qualitative feedback is uniformly positive (balanced rhythm, 10‑minute settle). AutoGluon predicts 6.86 for the overstimulation zone, slightly lower than the observed mean, suggesting a favorable real‑world effect. YouTube reach is relatively high (30 views) but retention data are missing.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Most users said this helped reduce overstimulation and felt balanced soon after.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) maintains 65 % positive outcomes. Listeners report 10‑minute settle times and mixed music opinions. AutoGluon’s overstimulation prediction (6.86) aligns well. YouTube views are low (5).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners experienced a steady calming effect for overstimulation.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) shows 64 % positive outcomes with a tight distribution. Qualitative comments note balanced rhythm and swift settlement. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, again modestly below observed. YouTube views=8.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: People reported a pleasant settling after a short listening period.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) yields a right‑skewed outcome distribution with 63 % positive ratings. Qualitative data reports rapid settlement (5‑10 min) and balanced rhythm perception. AutoGluon predicts a score of 7.84 for the anxiety zone, aligning closely with observed performance. The limited YouTube reach (7 views) suggests under‑exposure. Overall, the signal is robust but benefits from broader discovery.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt a strong, quick reduction in anxiety and rated the experience positively.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) shows tighter score clustering and 67 % positive outcomes. Listeners note a typical 10‑minute settle time and mixed music opinions. AutoGluon’s predicted anxiety score (≈7.8) is slightly above the observed mean, indicating modest room for improvement. YouTube views are scarce (6).
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners generally found this protocol calming and helpful for easing stress.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains a high mean with a broader spread. Qualitative reports emphasize a 10‑minute settle and strong “very settling” rhythm feedback. AutoGluon predicts around 7.5, modestly lower than observed. No YouTube data are available yet.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: People reported noticeable calmness after a short settle period.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) records 53 % positive outcomes, reflecting the acute‑distress baseline. Qualitative comments note quick settlement (5‑10 min) and balanced rhythm. AutoGluon predicts a higher crisis score (7.96) than observed, suggesting the protocol may be under‑performing relative to model expectations. YouTube reach is minimal (6 views).
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Despite being for crisis moments, many users noted some improvement fairly quickly.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) improves modestly to 55 % positive. Listeners highlight very settling rhythm and 10‑minute settle times. AutoGluon’s crisis prediction (7.96) exceeds the mean, flagging a potential efficacy gap. YouTube views total 4.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners felt a modest lift in distress, with a calming rhythm.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) shows similar performance to Harbor with 52 % positive. Qualitative data reveal rapid settlement (immediate to 5 min) for many users. AutoGluon predicts a crisis score of 7.96, again above observed, indicating room for refinement. YouTube views count 5.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: A mix of quick and slower improvements were reported during acute distress.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) records 65 % positive outcomes; listeners frequently settle within 5‑10 min and praise the rhythm. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for the sleep zone, close to the observed mean. YouTube views are minimal (4).
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners often felt sleepier and more relaxed within minutes.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) has the tightest HEALING spread (std = 1.55) and 67 % positive ratings. Clinical listener types dominate, with longer 10‑minute settle times. AutoGluon’s sleep prediction (7.54) matches the mean. YouTube views=5.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Most found it effective for sleep, though some needed a bit more time.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) yields 64 % positive outcomes. Settlement is split between 5‑ and 10‑minute intervals. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, consistent with observations. YouTube views=3.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Many reported better rest and a soothing rhythm.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) records the lowest focus mean (56 % positive) and high rhythm‑busyness reports. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for the focus zone, exceeding the observed mean, indicating potential under‑performance. YouTube views=5.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improvements were modest, with some noting the rhythm felt busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) improves to 66 % positive and shows a tighter score spread. AutoGluon’s focus prediction (7.73) is modestly above the observed mean, suggesting room for optimization. YouTube views=4.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Most experienced a clear focus boost and liked the music.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) attains the highest overall mean (71 % positive) but also the highest std, implying a bimodal responder pattern. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus, close to observed. YouTube views=15.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Many reported a significant focus gain, though experiences varied.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) shows 53 % positive outcomes for creative flow. Listeners report mixed settlement times and moderate rhythm balance. AutoGluon does not assign a specific cross‑bucket prediction, but the observed mean aligns with the model’s zone expectations. YouTube views=2.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow improved modestly for many listeners.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) improves consistency (std = 1.41) and 58 % positive outcomes. Qualitative data note strong “loved it” music feedback. AutoGluon prediction is close to observed. YouTube views=2.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Users felt a clearer creative boost with consistent rhythm.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) achieves the highest mean in the Creative Flow line with low variance. Listeners often report significant improvement. AutoGluon does not flag a discrepancy. YouTube views=7.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: A strong creative lift was noted by most participants.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) shows 64 % positive outcomes with long settle times (often 20 min). AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for maintenance, slightly above observed. YouTube views=3.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Listeners felt balanced and steady, often after a longer session.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) displays the highest variance in the maintenance line (std = 2.03) and 59 % positive outcomes. AutoGluon’s maintenance prediction (7.69) exceeds the mean, flagging possible heterogeneity. YouTube views=4.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Responses were mixed; some felt well‑balanced, others less so.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) records 71 % positive outcomes but a notable 22 % “did NOT settle” rate. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, above observed, suggesting entrainment gaps for a subset. YouTube views=3.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Most liked the balance, but a few did not feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) attains 54 % positive outcomes; qualitative data note long settle times (often 20 min) and strong “loved it” music feedback. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 for the low‑affect zone, substantially higher than observed, suggesting untapped potential. YouTube views=8.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood improved for many, especially those who loved the music.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) improves to 64 % positive with balanced rhythm perception. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, again above observed, indicating room for protocol optimization. YouTube views=8.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Most felt a lift in energy and mood.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) records the lowest mean in the mood‑energy line (56 % positive) and some “no effect” reports. AutoGluon’s low‑affect prediction (8.23) exceeds the mean, flagging potential under‑performance. YouTube views=4.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: The mood boost was modest, with several noting no change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) records the lowest positive rate (45 %) and high variance, with some high outliers. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 for motivation, well above the mean, indicating a likely mis‑match to listener intent. YouTube views=5.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation responses varied widely; some felt a boost, others not.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) shows 43 % positive outcomes and moderate spread. AutoGluon assigns the motivation zone prediction of 8.23, again exceeding observed performance. YouTube views=7.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) combines a high mean (62 % positive) with the largest std in the dataset, reflecting a split responder group. AutoGluon predicts 8.23, modestly above observed. YouTube views=7.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: A good number felt motivated, though experiences were uneven.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) yields 54 % positive outcomes; rhythm is broadly described as balanced. AutoGluon predicts 8.23 for the low‑affect zone, substantially higher than observed, indicating potential under‑utilization. YouTube views=8.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mixed feelings about mental clarity; some liked the rhythm, others felt neutral.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) achieves 56 % positive outcomes with strong “very settling” rhythm feedback. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for mind‑clarity, modestly above observed, suggesting possible improvement via rhythm tuning. YouTube views=12.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Many found it very calming for mental clarity.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) shows the lowest mean and highest dispersion, with a bimodal pattern (scores clustered at 0‑2 and 8‑9). AutoGluon predicts 8.23 for the low‑affect zone, dramatically higher than observed, flagging a serious mismatch. YouTube views=8.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results were split—some struggled while a few felt clear.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The dataset reveals generally positive listener outcomes with clear series‑level patterns and a handful of protocols that merit closer scrutiny.
• HEALING protocols cluster tightly (mean ≈ 7.0, lowest variance) indicating reliable entrainment.
• THRIVING shows the highest single‑protocol mean (T‑FC‑L3 = 7.63) but also elevated variability.
• TRANSFORMING displays the widest spread, highlighted by the low‑scoring X‑MC‑L3 (mean 5.7).
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
HEALING delivers consistent effects across its descent‑arc designs, while THRIVING’s hold‑arc yields higher peaks with more spread, and TRANSFORMING’s ascent‑arc shows the greatest variance as expected.
• HEALING maintains means ≥6.5 and tight stds, reflecting stable nervous‑system regulation.
• THRIVING achieves the highest observed means (focus line) but exhibits bimodal patterns in some protocols.
• TRANSFORMING includes both high (X‑MD‑L3 7.15) and low (X‑MC‑L3 5.7) outcomes, suggesting subgroup effects.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge from series expectations and may indicate population mismatches or design issues.
• X‑MC‑L3 (Cosmos) shows a low mean (5.7) with high std (2.45) and bimodal distribution.
• T‑FC‑L3 (Summit) records the highest mean (7.63) but also the highest std (1.97), hinting at a split responder group.
• T‑MB‑L3 (Orbit) reports a notable ‘did NOT settle’ rate (≈22%), suggesting entrainment failure for some listeners.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements are suggested for protocols with convergent signals across analysts.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MC‑L3 – redesign rhythm density or limit exposure; current low efficacy signals a mismatch.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑FC‑L3 – investigate audience segmentation and consider a longer hold phase to reduce outcome variance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑OS‑L1 – experiment with a slower ease‑in or extended duration to potentially boost its already strong performance.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H‑AX‑L1 – add a brief hold segment to test whether outcomes improve beyond the current high mean.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T‑MB‑L2 – conduct subgroup analysis to understand the high variance and tailor parameters accordingly.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X‑MD‑L1 – implement a short pre‑protocol questionnaire to screen for suitable listener profiles.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift and semantic clustering provide auxiliary context for protocol evolution.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: many protocols (e.g., T‑MB‑L3 sim = 0.604, H‑SL‑L1 sim = 0.659) exhibit notable drift, suggesting evolving response patterns.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: X‑ME series tightly clustered (sim > 0.95), confirming conceptual similarity; T‑CF‑L1 is analytically distinct.
• Confidence: drift metrics are based on limited runs; interpret with moderate caution.
